mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive returns occur in less than 55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 60% or more of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to yield a positive Sharpe ratio on 60-day forward returns over any 4-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production below -0.5% reflects immediate supply-side contraction that triggers short-term risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) during all identified supply contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a flat or rising federal funds rate indicates restrictive monetary policy that erodes real discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative out-of-sample R-squared or positive returns exceed 58% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate wage income and consumer spending capacity, mechanically supporting corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 60-day forward returns show a negative or zero correlation with payroll growth over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction exceeding -0.2% triggers immediate recession fears that force rapid portfolio de-risking, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of contraction quarters over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing consumer price inflation signals improving real output margins, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during disinflationary periods.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative alpha relative to a buy-and-hold benchmark in 70% of disinflationary periods.
